verse.arabic_text: وَقَضَىٰ رَبُّكَ أَلَّا تَعْبُdُآا۟ إِلَّآ إِيَّاهُ وَبِٱلْوَٰلِدَيْنِ إِحْسَـٰنًا
verse.translation: And your Lord has decreed that you worship none but Him, and to be good to your parents.

theme_question.correct: The inseparable duties of worshipping Allah alone and showing excellent kindness to parents.
options: The inseparable duties of worshipping Allah alone and showing excellent kindness to parents.
The specific rules of inheritance for elderly parents.
The obligation of financial charity towards all relatives.
The historical account of Prophet Ibrahim's relationship with his father.

educational_notes.verse_context: This verse is part of a series of foundational commandments in Surah Al-Isra that outline a comprehensive moral and social code for believers. It comes immediately after the prohibition of shirk (associating partners with Allah), placing the duty to parents at the highest level of importance right after the duty to God Himself.
educational_notes.theme_explanation: The theme is the profound and direct link between Tawhid (the Oneness of Allah) and 'Birr al-Walidayn' (excellent kindness to parents). By placing these two commands together in a single decree ('qada' - He has decreed), Allah elevates the status of parents and establishes that honoring them is not merely a social courtesy but a fundamental act of worship and obedience.
educational_notes.surah_overview: Surah Al-Isra, also known as Bani Isra'il (The Children of Israel), is a Meccan surah that begins by mentioning the miraculous Night Journey of Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) from Makkah to Jerusalem. The surah addresses the history of the Children of Israel as a lesson, and lays down essential principles of faith, morality, and social conduct, including the rights of parents, orphans, and the needy, and warns against arrogance and transgression.
